title: Use purged grouped temporal CV, then build only OOF-calibrated stacks on top of it
summary: This problem has both chronology and grouping.
rationale: This problem has both chronology and grouping. `TimeSeriesSplit` protects time order but ignores groups; `StratifiedGroupKFold` protects groups but not chronology. Any full-data frequency/target statistics or naive stacking can overstate local CV, and LightGBM explicitly warns that imbalance weighting harms probability quality.
implementation_hint: Evaluate with a stricter fold design such as leave-`Race-Year`-out or chronological race blocks with non-overlapping race groups, and recompute every count/target/stat feature inside each fold only. Build stacking features from OOF predictions only, then calibrate the final blend with isotonic or Platt on OOF scores rather than raw train predictions.
expected_effect: May lower local CV slightly but should improve leaderboard reliability and make ensemble gains trustworthy instead of leakage-driven.
risk: Fewer effective groups can make fold variance noisy; calibration can also reduce AUC if done on weak or unstable OOF predictions.
